title: HTML tags and markup
description: Angle brackets, attributes, closing tags — the web's basic syntax
text:
Hello, world!

Home

tips.text: A tag opens with < and closes with >. Markup is unforgiving - a missing bracket and the whole line falls apart.
The attributes live inside: class="box". Mind the quotes; they have to pair.
Every tag you open, you close - </tag>. Leave one hanging and the page knows.

tips.title: Note
tips.text: A few tags close themselves: stands alone, no closing tag needed.

character_tips.anna: Markup is just structure you can see. The < > / and " become second nature once you stop fearing them.
character_tips.maxim: People who build the web type these brackets all day. Make the shapes automatic.
character_tips.knopych: Open the tag, close the tag. Balanced and clean - that's the craft.
character_tips.klavochka: Tags wrap the words the way punctuation wraps a sentence. Tidy work, both.
